requirements.evidence: [android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSToolbar.kt:68] maps `toolbarHeight` to `space.xxxxl`, but `tokens/semantic/semantic.tokens.json:558-560` defines `space.4xl = 64` while `tokens/semantic/dimensions.tokens.json:144-147` defines `component.toolbarHeight = 56`. The strengthened test at [android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSToolbarTest.kt:46-60] compares against the same broken bridge, so it still passes without proving the required 56dp toolbar token.
requirements.remediation: Resolve toolbar height from the actual 56dp component token source on Android, not `space.xxxxl`, and update `default_render_uses_chrome_tokens` to assert the canonical 56dp value or a direct component-token-backed API so the mismatch cannot self-validate.

requirements.evidence: `title_only_and_two_actions_variants` remains in [android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSToolbarTest.kt:76-97], and the provided host validation reports `:app:testDebugUnitTest --tests "com.laneshadow.ui.molecules.LSToolbarTest"` passed on commit `86f24f890661764f15a7532899954ef4fe3e9d56`.

[android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSNavHeader.kt:135-143] renders the large title through `LSText(... TypographyVariant.Opinion.Lg ...)`, and [android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSNavHeaderTest.kt:31-49] still asserts `Opinion.Lg`. Provided host unit-test validation passed.

[android/app/src/test/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSNavHeaderTest.kt:53-79] now asserts exact `LSNavHeaderVerticalGapKey` and exact `LSNavHeaderSubtitleColorKey`, while [android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSNavHeader.kt:78-87,129-154] wires those semantics to `theme.space.xs` and tertiary subtitle text.

[android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSToolbar.kt:121-136] adds a `windowInsets` parameter defaulting to `WindowInsets.systemBars`, and [android/app/src/androidTest/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSToolbarUiTest.kt:31-75] compares injected top insets versus zero insets by exact height delta. Provided evidence log reports `:app:connectedDebugAndroidTest -Pandroid.testInstrumentationRunnerArguments.class=com.laneshadow.ui.molecules.LSToolbarUiTest` passed on `emulator-5554`.

Story IDs across `LSToolbarStory.kt` and `LSNavHeaderStory.kt` total `7`, and `rg -n "Color\(0xFF" android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSToolbar.kt android/app/src/main/java/com/laneshadow/ui/molecules/LSNavHeader.kt` returned no matches.

findings.evidence: The remediation changed the toolbar-height bridge to `space.xxxxl`, but the Android semantic space ladder still defines `4xl = 64`, not the required toolbar height of 56. This violates the molecule spec and also makes `LSNavHeader` consume the wrong toolbar row height through the same helper.
findings.fix: Expose or bridge the actual component toolbar-height token (`component.toolbarHeight = 56`) into the Android theme surface and use that for `toolbarComponentSizing.toolbarHeight`.

findings.evidence: `default_render_uses_chrome_tokens` derives `expectedToolbarHeight` from `theme.toolbarComponentSizing.toolbarHeight`, which is the same faulty helper used by production. The test therefore cannot detect the 64dp/56dp contract mismatch and does not really prove the exact token bridge.
findings.fix: Assert against the canonical 56dp toolbar token or a generated component-height token/API independent of `toolbarComponentSizing` so the test can fail when the bridge is wrong.

summary: NEEDS_FIXES: AC-2 through AC-6 are satisfied, but AC-1 still fails because the new Android toolbar-height bridge resolves to `space.4xl` (64dp) instead of the required 56dp component toolbar token, and the updated unit test self-validates that mistake.
